manifest.title: U.S.S. Glass Harbor Steamrunner-Class Ship Dataset

areas.name: Bridge
areas.exteriorPlacement: Inset forward between cowling structures rather than raised on a saucer crown
hardFacts: The bridge is inset into the forward hull geometry, not perched on a broad Galaxy-style saucer crown.
constraints: Bridge scenes should turn contested movement into accountable command decisions.

areas.name: Ready room and briefing room
areas.zone: Compact command support adjacent to or below the bridge
areas.exteriorPlacement: Forward command structure near bridge access
hardFacts: Command support rooms are compact and remain operationally close to the bridge.
constraints: Private command scenes should stay connected to ship traffic, recovery calls, and route pressure.

areas.name: Wardroom, mess, and crew commons
hardFacts: The wardroom is a working commons, not a luxury lounge or Ten Forward duplicate.
constraints: Crowding should change the room before abstract morale does.

areas.name: Sickbay and medical isolation
hardFacts: Sickbay is protected from direct flight-deck exposure while remaining connected to transporter and cargo casualty routes.
constraints: Medical capacity should expose limits; the ship cannot house every survivor or treat mass emergence alone.

areas.name: Transporter rooms, security, and armory
hardFacts: Transport, custody, and security spaces are close enough for movement to become accountable immediately.
constraints: Transporters require locks, biofilters, shield status, power, and custody decisions.

areas.name: Mission ops, route analysis, and science labs
hardFacts: Mission ops is the class-native place where a Steamrunner earns the right to say a route is safe enough.
constraints: Good sensors still produce uncertainty, blind spots, false confidence, and political exposure.

areas.name: Cargo, salvage, and recovery holds
hardFacts: Cargo and recovery holds make rescue, salvage, relief, and evidence physical.
constraints: Cargo volume runs out before compassion does.

areas.name: Main engineering and aft deflector control
areas.exteriorPlacement: Internal support for the aft detached deflector housing and cowled nacelle roots
hardFacts: Engineering and aft deflector control are physically and operationally tied to stern field geometry.
constraints: Deflector activity, tractor load, shields, and shuttle recovery can conflict under pressure.

areas.name: Aft-lower flight deck
areas.exteriorPlacement: Aft-lower service volume in the broad rear hull above and around the detached deflector housing; approach from astern and below
hardFacts: Steamrunner small-craft recovery uses an aft-lower stern service volume in Directive baseline.
Small craft approach from astern and below.
The aft deflector housing and shuttle recovery share stern geometry.
constraints: Do not depict a wide forward, saucer-underbelly, or generic belly shuttlebay.
Deflector activity, tractor load, shields, and bay force fields can affect recovery timing.

areas.name: Jefferies tubes and lower support
areas.zone: Lower maintenance network and cowling support
hardFacts: Lower support and maintenance access expose the ship as a body of heat, conduits, vibration, and narrow routes.
constraints: Maintenance scenes should use cramped physical risk rather than generic corridor action.

systems.name: Aft deflector and field geometry
systems.capability: Navigation and field projection from stern geometry, creating class-specific towing, hazard, and recovery constraints.

systems.name: Tractor, towing, and convoy control
systems.scope: Tractor emitters, shield arcs, and convoy movement control
systems.capability: Secures wrecks, guides convoys, holds recovery lines, and protects traffic one crisis at a time.

systems.name: Phasers, torpedoes, shields, and deterrence
systems.capability: Credible 2370s escort firepower and shielding for convoy cover, route denial, and self-defense.

systems.name: Sensors, route maps, and chart custody
systems.scope: Mission ops sensor fusion and route-record control
systems.capability: Turns contested space into maps, models, custody records, and command recommendations.

systems.name: Small-craft, cargo, medical, and security integration
systems.scope: Flight deck, cargo holds, transporter, medical, and security traffic chain
systems.capability: Moves field teams, survivors, cargo, evidence, and casualties through a constrained rescue chain.

payload.summary: U.S.S. Glass Harbor is a Steamrunner-class vessel. A Steamrunner-class ship is a compact 2370s Starfleet escort, route-security, recovery, and tactical survey vessel whose tactical profile is most useful when it keeps movement lawful, mapped, supplied, and alive. Campaign use: U.S.S. Glass Harbor: Nerine Reef route-security, gravitic survey, civilian rescue support, towing, and chart custody.
constraints: Do not give the class a generic forward deflector or Galaxy-style saucer layout.
Do not make tactical credibility erase route, rescue, custody, and convoy tradeoffs.
hardAnchors: Flattened irregular saucer and cowled nacelles.
Main deflector is aft on a detached secondary-hull housing.
Directive baseline places small-craft operations in an aft-lower service volume approached from astern and below.

payload.summary: The Steamrunner exterior reads broad, flat, tactical, and workmanlike: embedded nacelles, inset bridge, aft deflector housing, service locks, and recovery-facing stern geometry.
constraints: Exterior prose should respect the aft deflector and cowled nacelle geometry.
hardAnchors: The bridge is inset forward between cowling structures.
The aft deflector housing is a class-defining exterior feature.
Stern geometry matters for towing, field work, and shuttle recovery.
textures: shield arcs over a flattened hull

payload.summary: The aft-lower flight deck is the Steamrunner mission threshold, with shuttle approach from astern and below through stern service geometry. Steamrunner small-craft recovery uses an aft-lower stern service volume in Directive baseline. Small craft approach from astern and below. The aft deflector housing and shuttle recovery share stern geometry.
constraints: Do not depict a wide forward, saucer-underbelly, or generic belly shuttlebay.
Deflector activity, tractor load, shields, and bay force fields can affect recovery timing.
hardAnchors: Steamrunner small-craft recovery uses an aft-lower stern service volume in Directive baseline.
Small craft approach from astern and below.
The aft deflector housing and shuttle recovery share stern geometry.
avoid: Do not depict a wide forward, saucer-underbelly, or generic belly shuttlebay.

payload.summary: The Deck 1 bridge is the Steamrunner decision room where routes, shuttles, tractor loads, civilian traffic, and tactical arcs become one order.
constraints: Bridge scenes should turn contested movement into accountable command decisions.
hardAnchors: The bridge is inset into the forward hull geometry, not perched on a broad Galaxy-style saucer crown.

refs: 5.2 Ready room and briefing room, Decks 1-2

payload.summary: Command support spaces convert operational noise into private judgment and senior-staff synthesis.
constraints: Private command scenes should stay connected to ship traffic, recovery calls, and route pressure.
hardAnchors: Command support rooms are compact and remain operationally close to the bridge.

refs: 5.3 Wardroom, mess, and crew commons, Decks 3-4

payload.summary: The wardroom turns route policy, rescue outcomes, and crew fatigue into ordinary shipboard life.
constraints: Crowding should change the room before abstract morale does.
hardAnchors: The wardroom is a working commons, not a luxury lounge or Ten Forward duplicate.

payload.summary: Steamrunner sickbay turns route-security and recovery missions into bodies, quarantine risk, and triage math.
constraints: Medical capacity should expose limits; the ship cannot house every survivor or treat mass emergence alone.
hardAnchors: Sickbay is protected from direct flight-deck exposure while remaining connected to transporter and cargo casualty routes.

refs: 5.5 Transporter rooms, security, and armory, Deck 6

payload.summary: Transporter and security spaces turn intent into irreversible movement and accountable custody.
constraints: Transporters require locks, biofilters, shield status, power, and custody decisions.
hardAnchors: Transport, custody, and security spaces are close enough for movement to become accountable immediately.

cards.title: Mission ops, route analysis, and science labs

refs: 5.6 Mission ops, route analysis, and science labs, Decks 7-8

payload.summary: Mission ops makes contested routes legible enough for command action without making the map automatically true.
constraints: Good sensors still produce uncertainty, blind spots, false confidence, and political exposure.
hardAnchors: Mission ops is the class-native place where a Steamrunner earns the right to say a route is safe enough.

refs: 5.7 Cargo, salvage, and recovery holds, Decks 9-10

payload.summary: Cargo and recovery holds turn abstract rescue into mass, labels, custody, locks, and finite volume.
constraints: Cargo volume runs out before compassion does.
hardAnchors: Cargo and recovery holds make rescue, salvage, relief, and evidence physical.

cards.title: Main engineering and aft deflector control

refs: 5.8 Main engineering and aft deflector control, Decks 11-12

payload.summary: Engineering turns route promises into physics: power, tractor load, deflector timing, cowling heat, and repairable risk.
constraints: Deflector activity, tractor load, shields, and shuttle recovery can conflict under pressure.
hardAnchors: Engineering and aft deflector control are physically and operationally tied to stern field geometry.

payload.summary: The aft-lower flight deck is the Steamrunner mission threshold, with shuttle approach from astern and below through stern service geometry.
constraints: Do not depict a wide forward, saucer-underbelly, or generic belly shuttlebay.
Deflector activity, tractor load, shields, and bay force fields can affect recovery timing.
hardAnchors: Steamrunner small-craft recovery uses an aft-lower stern service volume in Directive baseline.
Small craft approach from astern and below.
The aft deflector housing and shuttle recovery share stern geometry.

refs: 5.10 Jefferies tubes and lower support, Decks 14-16

payload.summary: Jefferies tubes and lower support make the Steamrunner hull physically accountable.
constraints: Maintenance scenes should use cramped physical risk rather than generic corridor action.
hardAnchors: Lower support and maintenance access expose the ship as a body of heat, conduits, vibration, and narrow routes.

payload.summary: The aft deflector is the recurring Steamrunner system that makes field work, towing, and shuttle recovery physically specific.
constraints: Capability: Navigation and field projection from stern geometry, creating class-specific towing, hazard, and recovery constraints.
Dependencies: power transfer; tractor loads; shuttle recovery timing; stern shield arcs.
Failure modes: field instability; recovery window delay; tow interference; sensor distortion.
hardAnchors: Aft detached secondary-hull housing
Navigation and field projection from stern geometry, creating class-specific towing, hazard, and recovery constraints.

payload.summary: Tractor and convoy systems are strong but bounded by load, arcs, power, and what becomes exposed while the ship holds one line.
constraints: Capability: Secures wrecks, guides convoys, holds recovery lines, and protects traffic one crisis at a time.
Dependencies: tractor emitter availability; structural integrity; shield geometry; power reserve; legal custody.
Failure modes: emitter overheating; tow drift; shield exposure; custody dispute.
hardAnchors: Tractor emitters, shield arcs, and convoy movement control
Secures wrecks, guides convoys, holds recovery lines, and protects traffic one crisis at a time.

payload.summary: Steamrunner tactical systems create credible threat presence but should support escort and rescue rather than erase politics.
constraints: Capability: Credible 2370s escort firepower and shielding for convoy cover, route denial, and self-defense.
Dependencies: weapon arcs; shield sectors; rules of engagement; torpedo inventory; civilian proximity.
Failure modes: arc limitation; shield gap; political escalation; inventory pressure.
hardAnchors: Tactical and defensive suite
Credible 2370s escort firepower and shielding for convoy cover, route denial, and self-defense.

cards.title: Sensors, route maps, and chart custody

refs: 6.4 Sensors, route maps, and chart custody

payload.summary: The Steamrunner sensor suite makes movement legible while creating political exposure and false-certainty risk.
constraints: Capability: Turns contested space into maps, models, custody records, and command recommendations.
Dependencies: sensor calibration; probe telemetry; local interference; data custody; command authorization.
Failure modes: false certainty; blind spot; chart exposure; model drift.
hardAnchors: Mission ops sensor fusion and route-record control
Turns contested space into maps, models, custody records, and command recommendations.

cards.title: Small-craft, cargo, medical, and security integration

payload.summary: Flight deck, cargo, medical, and security are one chain; rescue scenes should feel connected rather than room-isolated.
constraints: Capability: Moves field teams, survivors, cargo, evidence, and casualties through a constrained rescue chain.
Dependencies: bay clearance; cargo volume; medical capacity; security routing; decontamination.
Failure modes: bay choke point; medical overflow; cargo conflict; security breach.
hardAnchors: Flight deck, cargo holds, transporter, medical, and security traffic chain
Moves field teams, survivors, cargo, evidence, and casualties through a constrained rescue chain.
